_doc: Worked example route-table for the per-seat cross-endpoint model router (src/adapter/model-router.mjs; rationale: docs/decisions/per-seat-model-routing.md). Copy it, set the env vars it names, then point Claude Code at the router: export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787. The router keys on each request body's `model` and forwards to the first route whose `match` glob matches. Keys are referenced by env-var NAME only — NEVER put a key value in this file (it is committed). Verified 2026-06-30 against the official DeepSeek docs (api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/anthropic_api): DeepSeek's Anthropic-compatible endpoint is https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic and authenticates with `x-api-key` (the same scheme Anthropic uses), so both routes below carry `x-api-key` and differ only in `keyEnv`.

_route_shape.match: glob over the model id; only `*` is a wildcard (e.g. claude-* / deepseek-*)
_route_shape.base_url: the backend origin (optionally with a base path, e.g. /anthropic); the client's request path is appended

auth.header: the header name to send the backend key under (e.g. x-api-key, or Authorization)
auth.keyEnv: the NAME of the env var holding the backend key — read at request time, never logged
auth.scheme: OPTIONAL prefix prepended to the key for the header value — e.g. "Bearer" yields `Authorization: Bearer <key>`; omit for a raw value like `x-api-key: <key>`
